event: A procession of peasants carrying a large crucifix
symbols: large crucifix carried aloft — the centrality of the cross in communal faith
peasants with scythes and tools — the working poor carrying both their faith and their labor
dark, somber clothing — the gravity of devotion, the weight of life
dense crowd pressing forward — the communal, collective nature of folk piety
description: A dense, dark composition filled with a crowd of grim-faced peasant men and women pressing forward in a procession. They wear dark brown and black clothing, hats, and headscarves. A large wooden crucifix with a golden corpus rises above the crowd at center, carried aloft. Several figures carry farming implements — scythes and tools — alongside the cross. The faces are weathered, stern, and deeply lined. The crowd fills the entire canvas edge to edge with almost no background visible.
theological_description: Egger-Lienz's powerful image of Tyrolean peasants carrying a crucifix in procession captures the deep-rooted folk piety of alpine Catholic communities. Though a modern painting, it visualizes the enduring scriptural call to take up the cross and follow Christ (Matthew 16:24). The peasants carrying their working tools alongside the crucifix embody the integration of daily labor and faith — the sanctification of ordinary life through devotion. The scythes they carry inevitably evoke the harvest imagery of Matthew 13:39 and Revelation 14:15, where the sickle is the instrument of final judgment. The grim determination on their faces reflects the 'narrow road' of Matthew 7:14 — faith as endurance rather than ease. The golden corpus shining above the dark, laboring crowd becomes a visual metaphor for the light shining in the darkness (John 1:5), the crucified Christ as the only source of hope amid the harshness of mortal life.

scripture_refs.reason: If anyone would come after me, let him take up his cross and follow me — the procession literally enacts this
The light shines in the darkness — the golden crucifix amid the dark crowd
The narrow road that leads to life — the grim determination of the faithful in their procession
